task_completion.description: Verify that the scconfig application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SeisComP configuration GUI (scconfig) window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the module management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'System' tab or the specific section displaying the list of SeisComP modules and their startup states is selected and active.

task_completion.description: Verify that the scautopick module is found in the list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'scautopick' module is clearly visible in the list of modules within the scconfig window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the scautopick module is enabled for automatic startup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle next to 'scautopick' (typically in an 'Auto' or 'Enable' column) is checked, indicating it is configured to start automatically.

task_completion.description: Verify that the configuration changes were saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' button is clicked, a 'Save configuration' confirmation dialog is visible, or the 'File' menu is open with the 'Save' option highlighted/selected.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the scconfig GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes to the module state must be done through the scconfig graphical interface, not by editing configuration files in a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal window should show the execution of commands like 'seiscomp enable scautopick' to achieve the goal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual scconfig application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window matches the standard appearance of SeisComP's scconfig tool, with appropriate menus, tabs, and module lists.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target module is 'scautopick'. The initial state is disabled. The expected state is enabled. The agent MUST use the 'scconfig' GUI application to enable the module for automatic startup and save the configuration.
